_description: Phase 2 Plan 01 Task 1: NACA 0012 forces, rotating-arm geometry, lambda parametric sweep
_units: SI throughout: m, m/s, N, W, dimensionless
naca_reference: NACA TR-824, Abbott, von Doenhoff & Stivers, 1945 (NASA NTRS ntrs.nasa.gov)
prandtl_ll_reference: Anderson, Fundamentals of Aerodynamics 5th ed., S5.3, Eqs. 5.61-5.62

LD_threshold_identity_note.question: What is the correct (L/D)_min threshold for F_tan > 0?
LD_threshold_identity_note.CONTEXT_formula_meaning: This is the kinematic ratio v_rel/v_tangential — NOT a force threshold.
LD_threshold_identity_note.why_different: cot(beta) = lambda vs sqrt(1+1/lambda^2) = v_rel/v_tan. At lambda=1: lambda=1.0, v_rel/v_tan=sqrt(2)=1.414. These are different quantities. The CONTEXT.md formula v_rel/v_tangential appears to be a misidentification: it would be the correct (L/D) threshold if F_tan were defined as L*cos(beta)-D*sin(beta) (i.e., if the force were decomposed along v_rel vs perpendicular to v_loop). With the stated convention F_tan = L*sin(beta) - D*cos(beta), the correct threshold is lambda.

lambda_0_3.note: lambda->0: beta->90deg, F_tan->L (full lift in tangential), P_shaft->0

lambda_5_0.note: lambda=5: beta=arctan(1/5)=11.31 deg; L/D_min=5.0; check whether NACA foil exceeds this

LD_threshold_identity_verification.note: At each lambda: sign(F_tan) must equal sign(L/D_3D - (L/D)_min). Mismatches indicate computation error.

2.0.note: P_shaft is r-independent at fixed lambda (F_tan*v_tan; r cancels)

2.5.note: P_shaft is r-independent at fixed lambda (F_tan*v_tan; r cancels)

3.0.note: P_shaft is r-independent at fixed lambda (F_tan*v_tan; r cancels)

3.66.note: P_shaft is r-independent at fixed lambda (F_tan*v_tan; r cancels)

pitfall_guards.fp-chain-loop-geometry: PASS: geometry_model=rotating_arm; v_tangential=lambda*v_loop; no chain-loop v_h
pitfall_guards.fp-LD-as-power-ratio: PASS: P_shaft = F_tan * v_tangential; no instance of L/D * P_drag
pitfall_guards.fp-fixed-v-vessel: PASS: v_loop = 3.7137 m/s loaded from JSON; not hardcoded 3.0 m/s
pitfall_guards.fp-2D-CL-no-correction: PASS: Prandtl LL applied at every force computation; C_L_2D never used raw
pitfall_guards.fp-mount-angle-as-AoA: PASS: AoA_eff = beta - mount_angle at every computation; velocity triangle used

verdict_note: CONTINUE: F_tan > 0 for at least one lambda in [0.3, 5.0] at AoA_target=7 deg. Proceed to Task 2 for ascending torque and Phase 1 anchor.
